label: Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs

node.label: Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs
node.definition: Cryptographic signatures and append-only logs authenticate artifacts, requests, model versions, and lineage events while making unauthorized modification detectable.
node.why_it_matters: They provide an integrity substrate for provenance, audit, and controlled agent execution.
limitations: Keys, identities, and trust roots must be managed securely, and signed data can still be false at creation time.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs has an `operationalizes` dependency on Distributed systems & networking because Distributed systems & networking is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs has an `operationalizes` dependency on Content credentials, signing & provenance because Content credentials, signing & provenance is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Cryptographic signing & tamper-evident logs has an `operationalizes` dependency on Algorithms & data structures because Algorithms & data structures is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

sentence_units.text: Cryptographic signatures and append-only logs authenticate artifacts, requests, model versions, and lineage events while making unauthorized modification detectable.
They provide an integrity substrate for provenance, audit, and controlled agent execution.
Keys, identities, and trust roots must be managed securely, and signed data can still be false at creation time.
